title: A sum of local flux differences telescopes to boundary fluxes

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Ch. 4, pp. 64-65.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any real-valued flux on natural-number grid indices, the sum from i=0 to n-1 of flux(i+1)-flux(i) equals flux(n)-flux(0).
assumptions: flux is a real-valued function on natural-number grid indices
the sum ranges over Finset.range n
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite sum of local flux differences equals the boundary flux difference.

declarations.name: fluxDifference
declarations.statement: def fluxDifference (flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := flux (i + 1) - flux i

declarations.name: fluxDifference_sum_range
declarations.statement: theorem fluxDifference_sum_range (flux : Nat -> Real) : forall n : Nat, Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => fluxDifference flux i) = flux n - flux 0

tasks.input: Translate the finite telescoping identity for local flux differences into Lean 4 using Finset.range.
tasks.target: theorem fluxDifference_sum_range (flux : Nat -> Real) : forall n : Nat, Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => fluxDifference flux i) = flux n - flux 0

tasks.input: Given fluxDifference and the theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by induction on n, using Finset.sum_range_succ and ring.

tasks.input: Find the Mathlib lemma that expands the sum over Finset.range (n+1).
